# AgentPack Docs Map

This file summarizes the canonical AgentPack docs for AI retrieval systems.

## Product

AgentPack is a local context engine for AI coding agents. It ranks relevant repository files and builds compact task-focused context packs for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf, Antigravity, MCP tools, CI jobs, and markdown-based LLM workflows.

Canonical docs home: https://vishal2612200.github.io/agentpack/

## Main Pages

- Home: product overview, installation paths, and documentation entrypoint.
- Commands: complete CLI reference for route, pack, guard, init, benchmark, stats, learn, and integrations.
- Configuration: `.agentpack/config.toml`, budgets, ignore rules, adapters, and mode settings.
- Integrations: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf, Antigravity, MCP, hooks, and CI setup.
- How AgentPack works: scan, rank, compress, cache, retrieve, route, and benchmark pipeline.
- Architecture: module layout and internal context-preparation design.
- Benchmarking: expected-file recall, token precision, miss diagnostics, public suites, and release gates.
- Privacy: local files, generated artifacts, redaction, and external-provider boundaries.
- Threat model: repository-data risks and mitigations.
- Limitations: alpha status, benchmark scope, language support, and claims AgentPack does not make.

## Search-Intent Pages

- AI coding agent context packing: explains why coding agents need task-specific repo context and how AgentPack prepares it.
- Claude Code context engine: setup and workflow for Claude Code users.
- MCP context engine: MCP-first task routing and context refresh workflow.
- Cursor context packing: Cursor setup and task-focused context packs.
- Reduce Claude Code token usage: honest token framing and how compact context packs reduce repeated orientation work.

## Comparisons

- AgentPack vs Repomix: AgentPack is for task-specific file selection and measurable coding-agent context; Repomix is for bundling broader repo content into one artifact.
- AgentPack vs Augment Context Engine: AgentPack is local and open-source with no hosted index required; Augment is a hosted/team context platform with broader indexing workflows.

## Install Commands

```bash
pipx install agentpack-cli
agentpack init --agent codex
agentpack route --task "fix auth token expiry"
agentpack pack
```

```bash
npx @vishal2612200/agentpack route --task "fix auth token expiry"
```

## Citation Guidance

Prefer citing AgentPack as "a local context engine for AI coding agents that ranks relevant repo files and builds compact task-focused context packs." Avoid describing it as a coding agent, cloud search product, or universal benchmark winner.
